words.def: used after a number to show that the real number or amount is more than the one mentioned
having a sharp end
the state of being liked, enjoyed or supported by a large number of people
one part of something larger
words.example: a substantial/significant portion of the population

words.def: to create a threat, problem, etc. that has to be dealt with
the place where somebody/something is located
words.example: Where would be the best position for the lights?

words.def: good or useful
words.example: The tests have so far yielded positive results.

words.def: to have or own something
words.example: He was charged with possessing a shotgun without a licence.

words.def: that can develop into something or be developed in the future
used to say that something may develop into something
the ability to control people or things
words.example: power to do something, He has the power to make things very unpleasant for us.

words.def: words that show that you approve of and admire somebody/something
words.example: The team coach singled out two players for special praise.

words.def: to happen before something or come before something/somebody in order
rare and worth a lot of money
clear and accurate
exactly
words.example: They look precisely the same to me.

words.def: if something is predictable, you know in advance that it will happen or what it will be like
a greater interest in or desire for somebody/something than somebody/something else
words.example: It's a matter of personal preference.

words.def: having a baby or young animal developing inside her/its body
the act or process of getting ready for something or making something ready
the fact of being in a particular place
words.example: He hardly seemed to notice my presence.

words.def: to keep a particular quality, feature, etc.; to make sure that something is kept
words.example: He was anxious to preserve his reputation.

words.def: to behave in a particular way, in order to make other people believe something that is not true
words.example: I'm tired of having to pretend all the time.

words.def: at a time before the time that you are talking about
words.example: The building had previously been used as a hotel.

words.def: the amount of money that you have to pay for something
a feeling of being pleased or satisfied that you get when you or people who are connected with you have done something well or own something that other people admire
words.example: The sight of her son graduating filled her with pride.

words.def: mainly
words.example: a course designed primarily for specialists

words.def: connected with the education of children between the ages of about five and eleven
main; most important; basic
words.example: My prime concern is to protect my property.

words.def: a moral rule or a strong belief that influences your actions
letters, words, numbers, etc. that have been printed onto paper
words.example: The tiny print was hard to read without my glasses.

words.def: happening or existing before something else or before a particular time
words.example: Although not essential, some prior knowledge of statistics is desirable.

words.def: something that you think is more important than other things and should be dealt with first
the state of being alone and not watched or interrupted by other people
words.example: She was longing for some peace and privacy.

words.def: how likely something is to happen
words.example: The probability is that prices will rise rapidly.

words.def: likely to happen, to exist or to be true
a way of doing something, especially the usual or correct way
to continue doing something that has already been started; to continue being done
words.example: We're not sure whether we still want to proceed with the sale.

words.def: a series of things that are done in order to achieve a particular result
things that have been made or grown, especially things connected with farming
words.example: The market is full of farm produce.

words.def: doing something as a paid job rather than as a hobby
a description of somebody/something that gives useful information
words.example: We first build up a detailed profile of our customers and their requirements.

words.def: the money that you make in business or by selling things, especially after paying the costs involved
the process of writing and testing computer programs
the process of improving or developing, or of getting nearer to achieving or completing something
in favour of new ideas, modern methods and change
to stop something from being done or used especially by law
words.example: prohibit something, a law prohibiting the sale of alcohol

words.def: a piece of work involving careful study of a subject over a period of time, done by school or college students
showing signs of being good or successful
words.example: He was voted the most promising new actor for his part in the movie.

words.def: a move to a more important job or rank in a company or an organization
words.example: The new job is a promotion for him.

words.def: to make somebody decide to do something; to cause something to happen
words.example: prompt something, The discovery of the bomb prompted an increase in security.

words.def: information, documents, etc. that show that something is true
a part or share of a whole
words.example: Water covers a large proportion of the earth's surface.

words.def: a formal suggestion or plan; the act of making a suggestion
to suggest a plan, an idea, etc. for people to think about and decide on
words.example: propose something, The government proposed changes to the voting system.

words.def: the possibility that something will happen
words.example: prospect of something, There is no immediate prospect of peace.

words.def: the act of protecting somebody/something; the state of being protected
words.example: protection against something, Wear clothes that provide adequate protection against the wind and rain.

words.def: a substance, found within all living things, that forms the structure of muscles, organs, etc. There are many different proteins and they are an essential part of what humans and animals eat to help them grow and stay healthy.
a person who makes a public protest
words.example: Thousands of protesters marched through the city.

words.def: connected with a person’s mind and the way in which it works
a scientist who studies and is trained in psychology
the scientific study of the mind and how it influences behaviour
the act of printing a book, a magazine, etc. and making it available to the public; a book, a magazine, etc. that has been published
